summary: Renamed several Kirby translation units and headers from mixed `ftKb_*` filenames to the project’s lower-case Kirby naming style, updating split configuration, build object paths, includes, and nearby comments. The PR was almost entirely mechanical rename fallout: `ftKb_Init.c/.h/.dox` became `ftkirby.c/.h/.dox`, and multiple Kirby copy-neutral-special files such as `ftKb_SpecialN.c`, `ftKb_SpecialNKp.c`, `ftKb_SpecialNLk.c`, `ftKb_SpecialNSs.c`, and `ftKb_SpecialNYs.c` became `ftkirbyspecialn.c`, `ftkirbyspecialkoopa.c`, `ftkirbyspeciallink.c`, `ftkirbyspecialsamus.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c`. No review discussion was present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` to point Kirby split entries at renamed source files while preserving the same address ranges and sections.
Updated `configure.py` object list entries for Kirby so build metadata uses the renamed translation units without changing their Matching/NonMatching status.
Renamed `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c` to `ftkirby.c` and changed its self-include from `ftKb_Init.h` to `ftkirby.h`.
Renamed `ftKb_Init.h` to `ftkirby.h` and `ftKb_Init.dox` to `ftkirby.dox` according to the diff excerpt.

Renamed several Kirby copied-neutral-special translation units from abbreviated `ftKb_SpecialN*` names to explicit lower-case fighter-target names: Koopa, Link, Samus, Yoshi, and base SpecialN are shown in the diff excerpt.

Replaced many include references to `ftKirby/ftKb_Init.h` or `ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.h` with `ftKirby/ftkirby.h` or `ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirby.h` across ftCommon, ft, item, player, and Kirby files.
Updated `ftKb_Init.static.h` to export `ftkirby.h` via the IWYU pragma instead of the old header name.
Updated a local documentation comment in `ftkirbyspecialn.c` from `External variables from ftKb_Init.c` to `External variables from ftkirby.c`.

A few formatting-only changes appear in the diff excerpt, such as brace/line wrapping in `ftCo_Attack100.c`, `ft_0C31.c`, and formatting of `void(HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(...)`; no semantic reason is documented in the slice.
smart_moves: The rename was propagated through both split metadata and build configuration, avoiding a common decomp rename failure where source paths compile but `splits.txt` still refers to stale TU names.
Includes were updated broadly across dependent systems instead of leaving compatibility shim headers, keeping the codebase aligned on a single canonical Kirby header name.
The renamed files preserve existing Matching/NonMatching classification in `configure.py`, indicating the PR did not conflate naming cleanup with matching-status changes.
The static header export was kept in sync with the public header rename, which is important for IWYU/export behavior and generated include dependency assumptions.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a translation unit in this repo, update all three layers together: source/header filenames, `configure.py` object paths, and `config/GALE01/splits.txt` split labels.
Mechanical TU renames should preserve address ranges in `splits.txt`; this PR only changes labels/paths in the excerpt, not section start/end addresses.

Header renames in fighter code often require updates outside the character directory because common fighter states, item code, player code, and core fighter code include character-specific declarations.
For decomp hygiene, comments that reference old source filenames should be updated along with the files, as shown by the `ftKb_Init.c` to `ftkirby.c` comment change.
The PR demonstrates a narrow cleanup style: keep matching status, code structure, and symbols stable while normalizing filenames.
naming_conventions: Kirby TU filenames were normalized away from mixed-case abbreviated `ftKb_*` filenames toward lower-case `ftkirby*` names.
`ftKb_Init.c/.h/.dox` became the simpler fighter root module name `ftkirby.c/.h/.dox`.
Copied-neutral-special TUs use explicit target fighter names in lower-case filenames, e.g. `ftkirbyspecialkoopa.c`, `ftkirbyspeciallink.c`, `ftkirbyspecialsamus.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c`.

The rename aligns with already-existing files in the same directory such as `ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c`, `ftkirbyspecialfox.c`, `ftkirbyspecialness.c`, `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`, `ftkirbyspecialpurin.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` shown in the changed-file list/diff.
Include paths should use the canonical renamed header `ftKirby/ftkirby.h` rather than the old `ftKirby/ftKb_Init.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic is documented; the provided evidence shows path/name-only changes.
The build configuration retained existing `Object(Matching, ...)` and `Object(NonMatching, ...)` classifications while changing filenames, which is the relevant matching-preservation tactic.
For split-aware matching work, a TU rename should not alter section address ranges unless the code is actually being resplit; the excerpt shows the same ranges under new filenames.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was provided; conclusions are based on the title, file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish renaming remaining `ftKb_SpecialNFx` or `ftkirbyspecialzelda` inconsistencies, if any.
Search for stale includes or comments containing `ftKb_Init` after PR 2602.
Search for other character TU rename PRs that update `splits.txt` and `configure.py` together.
Check whether the formatting-only hunks in `ftCo_Attack100.c` and `ft_0C31.c` were intentional formatter fallout or incidental.
